question_text: Look at the distance-time graph below. Between which two points is the object moving fastest? Point A to B, Point B to C, or Point C to D? Explain your answer.
correct_answer: Point A to B. The gradient (slope) of the line is steepest between A and B, which means the object covers more distance in the same amount of time, so it is moving fastest.
explanation: On a distance-time graph, the steeper the gradient, the faster the object is moving. A steeper line means greater distance covered in a shorter time interval, indicating higher speed.
